title: senecio variabilis
intro: Senecio variabilis, also known as Groundsel Bush or Ragwort, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Senecio variabilis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
